opinion.h1: LINCOLN BUILDING ASSOCIATES v. BARR, 355 U.S. 12 (1957)
opinion.h4: LINCOLN BUILDING ASSOCIATES v. BARR ET AL., DOING BUSINESS AS SWIM FOR HEALTH ASSOCIATION. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 128. Decided October 14, 1957.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
355 U.S. 12 COTTRELL v. PAWCATUCK COMPANY (FORMERLY C. B. COTTRELL & SONS CO.) ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF DELAWARE. No. 316. Decided October 14, 1957.
Henry M. Canby for the Pawcatuck Company et al. and David F. Anderson for the Harris-Seybold Company et al., appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
